data.content:
# Awesome Claude Skills

This repository provides a curated collection of over 40 Claude Skills designed to extend Claude's capabilities across Claude.ai, Claude Code, and the Claude API. Skills are modular, self-contained packages that provide specialized knowledge, workflows, and tools to transform Claude from a general-purpose assistant into a domain-specific expert. Each skill consists of a SKILL.md file with YAML frontmatter metadata plus optional bundled resources like scripts, reference documentation, and assets.

The collection spans nine major categories: Document Processing, Development & Code Tools, Data & Analysis, Business & Marketing, Communication & Writing, Creative & Media, Productivity & Organization, Collaboration & Project Management, and Security & Systems. Skills use a progressive disclosure design with three loading levels: metadata (always in context), SKILL.md body (when triggered), and bundled resources (loaded as needed). This architecture enables Claude to access specialized capabilities efficiently while managing context window constraints. The repository includes both official Anthropic skills and community-contributed skills covering real-world use cases from frontend development to security analysis, business automation to creative workflows.

## Core APIs and Functions

### Skill Initialization Script

Creates a new skill directory structure with template files and resource directories.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Usage: python3 skill-creator/scripts/init_skill.py <skill-name> --path <output-directory>

from pathlib import Path
import sys

# Initialize a new skill
python3 skill-creator/scripts/init_skill.py my-custom-skill --path ./

# Output:
# ✅ Created skill directory: ./my-custom-skill
# ✅ Created SKILL.md
# ✅ Created scripts/ directory
# ✅ Skill ready for development
```

### Skill Packaging and Validation

Validates skill structure and packages it into a distributable format.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Usage: python3 skill-creator/scripts/package_skill.py <path/to/skill-folder> [output-directory]

from pathlib import Path
import zipfile
from quick_validate import validate_skill

# Validate skill structure
python3 skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py ./my-custom-skill

# Package a skill after validation
python3 skill-creator/scripts/package_skill.py ./my-custom-skill ./dist

# Output:
# 🔍 Validating skill...
# ✅ Skill validation passed
# 📦 Packaging skill...
#   Added: my-custom-skill/SKILL.md
#   Added: my-custom-skill/scripts/
# ✅ Successfully packaged skill to: ./dist/my-custom-skill.zip
```

### Skill YAML Frontmatter Structure

Required metadata for every skill in SKILL.md.

```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Complete explanation of what the skill does and when Claude should use it. Include specific scenarios, file types, or tasks that trigger it. Use third-person (e.g., "This skill should be used when...").
license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt
---

# Skill Name

## Overview
[1-2 sentences explaining what this skill enables]

## When to Use This Skill
- Use case 1
- Use case 2

## Instructions
[Detailed instructions for Claude on how to execute this skill]
```

### Installing Skills in Claude.ai

Load skills through the Claude.ai web interface.

```bash
# Option 1: Browse the Skills Marketplace
# 1. Click the skill icon (🧩) in your chat interface
# 2. Browse available skills by category
# 3. Click to add skills to your conversation

# Option 2: Upload Custom Skills
# 1. Click the skill icon (🧩)
# 2. Select "Upload Custom Skill"
# 3. Upload the skill's SKILL.md file or zipped folder
# 4. Skill is now available in your conversation
```

### Installing Skills in Claude Code

Load skills into Claude Code by placing them in the configuration directory.

```bash
# Create skills directory
mkdir -p ~/.config/claude-code/skills/

# Copy skill to configuration directory
cp -r skill-name ~/.config/claude-code/skills/

# Verify skill metadata
head ~/.config/claude-code/skills/skill-name/SKILL.md

# Start Claude Code (skill loads automatically)
claude
```

### Using Skills via Claude API

Programmatically load and manage skills through the API.

```python
import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key="your-api-key")

# Use a skill in API call
response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
    skills=["skill-id-here"],
    messages=[
        {
            "role": "user",
            "content": "Analyze this PDF and extract all tables"
        }
    ],
    max_tokens=4096
)

# Access response
print(response.content[0].text)
```

### Document Skills - PDF Processing

Extract text, merge documents, and manipulate PDFs using Python libraries.

```python
from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter
import pdfplumber

# Extract text from PDF
reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
text = ""
for page in reader.pages:
    text += page.extract_text()

# Merge multiple PDFs
writer = PdfWriter()
for pdf_file in ["doc1.pdf", "doc2.pdf", "doc3.pdf"]:
    reader = PdfReader(pdf_file)
    for page in reader.pages:
        writer.add_page(page)

with open("merged.pdf", "wb") as output:
    writer.write(output)

# Extract tables from PDF
with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    for page in pdf.pages:
        tables = page.extract_tables()
        for table in tables:
            print(table)
```

### Document Skills - DOCX Processing

Create, edit, and analyze Word documents with tracked changes.

```python
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Inches, RGBColor
from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH

# Create new document
doc = Document()

# Add formatted content
title = doc.add_heading('Report Title', level=1)
title.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER

paragraph = doc.add_paragraph('Introduction text with ')
run = paragraph.add_run('bold emphasis')
run.bold = True

# Add table
table = doc.add_table(rows=3, cols=3)
table.style = 'Light Grid Accent 1'
for i, row in enumerate(table.rows):
    for j, cell in enumerate(row.cells):
        cell.text = f'Cell {i},{j}'

# Add image
doc.add_picture('chart.png', width=Inches(4))

# Save document
doc.save('report.docx')
```

### Document Skills - XLSX Processing

Spreadsheet manipulation with formulas, charts, and data transformations.

```python
import openpyxl
from openpyxl.chart import BarChart, Reference

# Create new workbook
wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
ws = wb.active

# Add data
ws['A1'] = 'Product'
ws['B1'] = 'Sales'
data = [
    ['Product A', 100],
    ['Product B', 150],
    ['Product C', 200]
]
for row in data:
    ws.append(row)

# Add formula
ws['B5'] = '=SUM(B2:B4)'

# Create chart
chart = BarChart()
data = Reference(ws, min_col=2, min_row=1, max_row=4)
cats = Reference(ws, min_col=1, min_row=2, max_row=4)
chart.add_data(data, titles_from_data=True)
chart.set_categories(cats)
ws.add_chart(chart, "D2")

# Save workbook
wb.save('sales_report.xlsx')
```

### Artifacts Builder Skill

Build complex React artifacts for Claude.ai using modern frontend technologies.

```bash
# Initialize a new React project with Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui
bash artifacts-builder/scripts/init-artifact.sh my-dashboard
cd my-dashboard

# Project structure created:
# ├── index.html
# ├── src/
# │   ├── App.tsx
# │   ├── components/ui/ (40+ shadcn/ui components)
# │   └── lib/utils.ts
# ├── package.json
# └── .parcelrc

# Develop your artifact by editing source files
# Edit src/App.tsx with your React components

# Bundle to single HTML file for sharing
bash artifacts-builder/scripts/bundle-artifact.sh

# Output: bundle.html (self-contained artifact with inlined JS/CSS)
# Can be shared directly in Claude.ai conversations

# Design Guidelines:
# - Avoid "AI slop" patterns (excessive purple gradients, uniform rounded corners, Inter font)
# - Use diverse layouts and color schemes
# - Follow modern design principles
```

### MCP Server Development Workflow

Create Model Context Protocol servers to integrate external services with LLMs.

```python
# Phase 1: Load MCP protocol documentation
from web_fetch import fetch_content

# Fetch latest MCP specification
mcp_docs = fetch_content("https://modelcontextprotocol.io/llms-full.txt")

# Phase 2: Implement MCP server with Python FastMCP
from mcp import FastMCP
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

mcp = FastMCP("github-integration")

class SearchReposInput(BaseModel):
    query: str = Field(description="Search query for repositories")
    sort: str = Field(default="stars", description="Sort by: stars, forks, updated")
    max_results: int = Field(default=10, ge=1, le=100)

@mcp.tool(
    readOnlyHint=True,
    openWorldHint=True
)
async def search_repositories(input: SearchReposInput) -> str:
    """
    Search GitHub repositories by query.

    Returns JSON formatted list of repositories with name, description, stars, and URL.
    """
    # Implementation with error handling
    try:
        results = await github_api.search_repos(
            query=input.query,
            sort=input.sort,
            max_results=input.max_results
        )
        return format_results(results, format="json")
    except RateLimitError as e:
        return f"Rate limit exceeded. Try again in {e.retry_after} seconds."

# Phase 3: Run server
if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run()

# Phase 4: Create evaluations (see evaluation.md in mcp-builder/reference/)
# Design test cases that verify tool effectiveness with real-world scenarios
```

### Business Automation Skills

Tools for marketing, lead research, and competitive analysis.

```python
# Lead Research Assistant - Identify and qualify leads
# 1. Analyze your product/service
# 2. Search for target companies matching ideal customer profile
# 3. Extract key information (decision makers, tech stack, recent news)
# 4. Provide actionable outreach strategies

# Domain Name Brainstormer - Generate domain ideas
from domain_checker import check_availability

domains = generate_domain_ideas("AI productivity tool")
# Checks availability across .com, .io, .dev, .ai TLDs
available = [d for d in domains if check_availability(d)]

# Competitive Ads Extractor - Analyze competitor ads
# Extracts ads from Meta Ad Library, Google Ads Transparency
# Analyzes messaging patterns and creative approaches
# Identifies what resonates with target audience

# Brand Guidelines - Apply consistent branding
# Uses Anthropic's official colors and typography
# Ensures professional design standards across artifacts
# Available for slides, docs, reports, landing pages
```

### Creative & Media Skills

Tools for design, image enhancement, and visual content creation.

```python
# Canvas Design - Create visual art in PNG and PDF
# Uses design philosophy and aesthetic principles
# Suitable for posters, designs, and static visual pieces

# Image Enhancer - Improve image quality
from PIL import Image, ImageEnhance, ImageFilter

def enhance_image(input_path, output_path):
    img = Image.open(input_path)
    # Enhance resolution and sharpness
    img = img.resize((img.width * 2, img.height * 2), Image.LANCZOS)
    enhancer = ImageEnhance.Sharpness(img)
    img = enhancer.enhance(2.0)
    img.save(output_path, quality=95)

# Slack GIF Creator - Create animated GIFs
# Optimized for Slack's size constraints (< 1MB recommended)
# Includes validators and composable animation primitives
# Perfect for custom reactions and team communication

# Theme Factory - Apply professional themes
# 10 pre-set themes: Midnight Galaxy, Ocean Depths, Desert Rose, etc.
# Consistent fonts and color schemes across all artifacts
# Supports slides, documents, reports, HTML landing pages
```

### Communication & Writing Skills

Tools for content creation, research, and meeting analysis.

```python
# Content Research Writer - Research-driven content creation
# 1. Conducts research on topics
# 2. Adds citations and references
# 3. Improves hooks and introductions
# 4. Provides section-by-section feedback

# Meeting Insights Analyzer - Analyze meeting transcripts
from meeting_analyzer import analyze_patterns

insights = analyze_patterns(transcript)
# Returns:
# - Speaking time ratios
# - Conflict avoidance patterns
# - Filler word usage
# - Leadership style indicators
# - Behavioral patterns

# Internal Comms - Write internal communications
# Templates for:
# - Company newsletters
# - 3rd party vendor updates
# - FAQ answers
# - Status reports
# - Project updates
# Uses company-specific formats and tone
```

### Productivity & Organization Skills

Tools for file management, invoices, and automation.

```bash
# File Organizer - Intelligently organize files
# - Understands file context beyond extensions
# - Finds duplicates and suggests better structures
# - Proposes organization schemes based on content

# Invoice Organizer - Tax preparation automation
# Reads invoices/receipts and extracts:
# - Date, vendor, amount, category
# - Renames consistently: YYYY-MM-DD_Vendor_Amount.pdf
# - Organizes by tax year and category

# Raffle Winner Picker - Cryptographically secure random selection
import secrets

def pick_winners(participants, num_winners=1):
    """Select random winners using secrets module"""
    return secrets.SystemRandom().sample(participants, num_winners)

# Supports:
# - CSV files
# - Spreadsheets
# - Google Sheets integration
# - Ensures fairness with cryptographic randomness
```

### Development Tools & Workflows

Advanced development skills for testing, CI/CD, and code quality.

```bash
# Changelog Generator - Automatic release notes
# Analyzes git commit history
# Transforms technical commits into user-facing changelog
# Groups by features, fixes, breaking changes

git log --pretty=format:"%h %s" v1.0.0..HEAD | \
  python changelog-generator/generate.py > CHANGELOG.md

# Webapp Testing - Automated browser testing with Playwright
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

with sync_playwright() as p:
    browser = p.chromium.launch()
    page = browser.new_page()
    page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
    page.screenshot(path="homepage.png")
    browser.close()

# Developer Growth Analysis - Track coding patterns
# Analyzes Claude Code chat history
# Identifies patterns and areas for growth
# Sends personalized reports to Slack

# Skill Share - Distribute skills via Slack
# Creates skills and automatically shares
# Uses Rube integration for notifications
# Enables team-wide skill distribution
```

### Security & Systems Skills

Tools for forensics, threat hunting, and security analysis.

```bash
# Computer Forensics - Digital forensics analysis
# Investigation techniques for digital evidence
# File system analysis and recovery
# Timeline reconstruction

# Metadata Extraction - Extract file metadata
exiftool document.pdf
# Returns: author, creation date, software used, edit history

# Threat Hunting with Sigma Rules - Security event analysis
# Uses Sigma detection rules for threat hunting
# Analyzes logs for security indicators
# Identifies potential threats and anomalies

# File Deletion - Secure data sanitization
shred -vfz -n 10 sensitive_file.txt
# Overwrites file 10 times before deletion
# Ensures data cannot be recovered
```

### Skill Directory Structure

Organize skill resources using the standard directory layout.

```bash
skill-name/
├── SKILL.md                    # Required: Instructions and metadata
├── scripts/                    # Optional: Executable code
│   ├── helper_script.py       # Python scripts for automation
│   └── process_data.sh        # Shell scripts for tasks
├── references/                 # Optional: Documentation loaded as needed
│   ├── api_reference.md       # API documentation
│   ├── schema.md              # Database schemas
│   └── workflow_guide.md      # Detailed procedures
├── templates/                  # Optional: Document templates
│   ├── report_template.docx   # Word templates
│   └── slide_deck.pptx        # PowerPoint templates
└── assets/                     # Optional: Files used in output
    ├── logo.png               # Images and icons
    └── boilerplate/           # Starter code directories

# Progressive disclosure loading:
# Level 1: name + description (always in context, ~100 words)
# Level 2: SKILL.md body (when skill triggers, <5k words)
# Level 3: Bundled resources (loaded as needed, unlimited)
```

### Skill Creator Best Practices

Step-by-step process for creating effective skills.

```python
# Step 1: Understand skill requirements with concrete examples
questions = [
    "What functionality should this skill support?",
    "Can you give examples of how it will be used?",
    "What would trigger this skill?",
    "Who is the target audience?"
]

# Step 2: Plan reusable contents
reusable_resources = {
    "scripts": ["rotate_pdf.py"],       # Repeated operations
    "references": ["api_docs.md"],      # Documentation to load
    "templates": ["report.docx"],       # Document templates
    "assets": ["logo.png"]              # Files for output
}

# Step 3: Initialize skill
bash_command = "python3 skill-creator/scripts/init_skill.py pdf-processor --path ./"

# Step 4: Edit SKILL.md with clear structure
skill_sections = {
    "overview": "What the skill enables (1-2 sentences)",
    "when_to_use": "Specific trigger scenarios (bullets)",
    "instructions": "Step-by-step procedures for Claude"
}

# Step 5: Test across platforms
platforms = ["claude.ai", "claude-code", "api"]
# Verify skill triggers correctly and produces expected outputs

# Step 6: Package and validate
package_command = "python3 skill-creator/scripts/package_skill.py ./pdf-processor ./dist"

# Step 7: Iterate based on real usage feedback
# Monitor how Claude uses the skill
# Refine descriptions and instructions
# Add examples for edge cases
```

### Evaluation Framework for MCP Servers

Create comprehensive evaluations to test MCP server effectiveness.

```xml
<!-- evaluations.xml -->
<evaluation>
  <qa_pair>
    <question>Find discussions about AI model launches with animal codenames. One model needed a specific safety designation that uses the format ASL-X. What number X was being determined for the model named after a spotted wild cat?</question>
    <answer>3</answer>
  </qa_pair>
  <qa_pair>
    <question>Search for repositories implementing neural network quantization. Which repository has the most stars and what compression ratio does it achieve for 8-bit models?</question>
    <answer>pytorch/ao with 4x compression</answer>
  </qa_pair>
</evaluation>
```

```python
# Run evaluations against MCP server
# Evaluation requirements:
# - Independent: Not dependent on other questions
# - Read-only: Only non-destructive operations
# - Complex: Requires multiple tool calls and reasoning
# - Realistic: Based on actual use cases
# - Verifiable: Single clear answer
# - Stable: Answer won't change over time

# Evaluation best practices:
# - Design evaluations before implementing tools
# - Include edge cases and error conditions
# - Test with realistic data volumes
# - Verify error handling and rate limiting
```

### Contributing Skills to Repository

Submit new skills following the contribution workflow.

```bash
# 1. Fork and create branch
git checkout -b add-skill-name

# 2. Create skill using template
python3 skill-creator/scripts/init_skill.py my-skill --path ./

# 3. Implement skill following structure
cat << 'EOF' > ./my-skill/SKILL.md
---
name: my-skill
description: This skill should be used when [describe specific scenario]. [Explain what it does and when to use it in third-person.]
license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt
---

# My Skill

[Brief overview of what this skill enables]

## When to Use This Skill
- Bullet point use case 1
- Bullet point use case 2
- Bullet point use case 3

## Instructions
[Detailed step-by-step instructions for Claude on how to execute this skill]

## Examples
[Real-world examples showing the skill in action]
EOF

# 4. Add to README.md in alphabetical order under appropriate category
# Example: Under "### Productivity & Organization"
# - [My Skill](./my-skill/) - One-sentence description.

# 5. Test skill across platforms
# - Claude.ai: Upload and test in conversation
# - Claude Code: Copy to ~/.config/claude-code/skills/ and test
# - API: Test via skills parameter in API call

# 6. Validate and commit
python3 skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py ./my-skill
git add .
git commit -m "Add my-skill for [specific use case]"
git push origin add-skill-name

# 7. Open Pull Request with:
# - Title: "Add [Skill Name] skill"
# - Description: Explain real-world use case and value
# - Attribution/inspiration source (if applicable)
# - Confirmation that skill is tested and validated
```

## Summary and Integration

Awesome Claude Skills provides a comprehensive ecosystem of over 40 modular skills spanning nine major categories: Document Processing, Development Tools, Data Analysis, Business & Marketing, Communication, Creative Media, Productivity, Collaboration, and Security. The repository serves multiple audiences: end users can discover and use pre-built skills for specific tasks, developers can create custom skills for domain-specific workflows, and organizations can codify internal knowledge and procedures. Skills work consistently across all Claude platforms (Claude.ai, Claude Code, and API), making workflows portable and standardized across the entire Claude ecosystem.

Integration patterns include browsing the Skills Marketplace in Claude.ai, installing skills in Claude Code via the configuration directory, and invoking skills programmatically through the Claude API with the skills parameter. The skill-creator tools provide complete scaffolding with initialization scripts, validation utilities, and packaging tools. Advanced use cases include creating MCP servers to integrate external APIs and services, developing complex React artifacts with modern frontend stacks, implementing specialized document processing workflows, and building business automation pipelines. The progressive disclosure architecture (metadata → SKILL.md → bundled resources) ensures efficient context management while providing deep capabilities through scripts, references, templates, and assets that load only when needed. Community contributions extend the ecosystem with tools like Skill Seekers (auto-converting documentation to skills) and platform-specific integrations (AWS, NotebookLM, Playwright), demonstrating the extensibility and real-world applicability of the skills framework.
